description:
You are acting as a security-focused log analyst who wants every
    investigation to take place inside an isolated Python environment.
    Your goal is to prepare that environment and capture proof that it
    was created correctly.

    Required final state of the system
    ----------------------------------
    1. A directory called /home/user/log_review_env must exist and
       represent a functional Python virtual environment created with
       the built-in venv module.  At minimum it must contain:
       • /home/user/log_review_env/bin/activate
       • /home/user/log_review_env/lib/              (the site-packages
         will be somewhere under here)
       • /home/user/log_review_env/pyvenv.cfg

    2. Inside that same directory there must be a text file named
       /home/user/log_review_env/setup.log.
       • The file must contain exactly one line (no blank lines before
         or after it).
       • That single line must be the verbatim stdout produced by the
         command:
             python -m pip --version
         executed *from inside* the newly activated virtual environment.
       • The format of that line must therefore start with the word
         “pip ”, followed by three space-separated fields that look like
         this example pattern:
             pip 23.0.1 from /home/user/log_review_env/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pip (python 3.10)
         Do not add any other text, prefix, or suffix.

    3. All of the above must be achieved with **one single compound
       shell command** executed in the terminal (you can chain sub-
       commands with &&, ; or a subshell – the point is that the user
       only presses Enter once).

    4. Do **not** use sudo or any other privilege escalation.

    What the automated checker will verify
    --------------------------------------
    • Directory /home/user/log_review_env exists.
    • File /home/user/log_review_env/setup.log exists and is not empty.
    • setup.log contains exactly one newline-terminated line that
      matches the regular expression
          ^pip [0-9]+\.[0-9.]* from /home/user/log_review_env/.+ \(python [0-9.]+\)\n$
    • After sourcing /home/user/log_review_env/bin/activate, running
      “python -m pip --version” prints a line identical to the one
      stored in setup.log.

    Your turn
    ---------
    Using a single terminal command, create the virtual environment,
    activate it, run the pip version check, and redirect that output
    into the required log file while leaving no extra lines.  When you
    are done, the checker should pass all of the above bullet points.
truth:
The automated tests will:
    1. Assert that the directory /home/user/log_review_env exists and
       contains:
         • bin/activate (file, any size)
         • lib/          (directory)
         • pyvenv.cfg    (file)
    2. Read /home/user/log_review_env/setup.log and ensure it contains
       exactly one newline-terminated line.
    3. Capture the line from setup.log (call it LOG_LINE).
    4. Run:
          source /home/user/log_review_env/bin/activate
          PY_LINE="$(python -m pip --version)"
       and check that LOG_LINE == PY_LINE.
    5. Fail if any additional files other than those created by venv
       and setup.log are present in /home/user/log_review_env.
